source_target: IN THE ARBITRATION BETWEEN HPFFA, LOCAL 341 AND CITY OF HOUSTON -- Opinion and Award, AAA Case No. 01-25-0005-2917, Consolidated CBA Grievances (Three Percent Pay Escalator; sick-reserve time-bar), Arbitrator William E. Hartsfield, decided 2026-02-27
reason: Downloaded and text-extracted (44,958 chars, clean text layer via ingest/extract_text.py). Confirms real parties (HPFFA Local 341, City of Houston Legal Department, Fire Chief Tom Munoz), a named AAA arbitrator (William E. Hartsfield), a formal 'final and binding Award per CBA Article 14' ruling ('the Grievance is sustained'), and explicit repeated reference to the '2024-2029 Collective Bargaining Agreement' ratified by City Council on/about June 18, 2024 -- matching independent news reporting (ABC13: 'Arbitrator sides with Houston firefighters union to enforce contractual obligations', describing the identical S.B. 916/new-revenue/three-percent-escalator dispute). This is grievance/contract-interpretation arbitration under the CBA's own Article 14 procedure, NOT Sec.174.1535 compulsory interest arbitration -- the award interprets and enforces an already-executed CBA's wage-escalator clause (Article 17 Sec.2), it does not set the CBA's original terms.
caveat: Hosted on hfdcoa.org, the official website of the Houston Fire Department Chief Officers Association -- a real, distinct, legitimate professional association for chief officers, NOT the same union as HPFFA Local 341 (which represents rank-and-file firefighters) and not HPFFA's or the City's own official site. Treated as official/high-quality on the strength of (a) internal consistency of case numbers/dates/real Houston officials' names/addresses, and (b) independent ABC13 news corroboration of the ruling's substance -- not because hfdcoa.org is itself a primary party.
notes: This is the primary document for the tx_houston_fire_2024 row (source_type=arbitration_award). Explicitly incorporates the Settlement Agreement (see next row) as 'Attachment 1... incorporated by this reference for all purposes as if fully set out in this Award' -- both are fetched as parts of one consolidated grievance-resolution instrument, per the 'clearly parts of the same operative agreement, both necessary' exception.

source_target: HPFFA v. City of Houston, Case No. 01-25-0005-2917 -- Settlement Agreement and Release (FY26 monetary allocations: laundry-service reallocation, holiday buy-back, uniform allowance, sick-reserve DCO/RE utilization)
reason: Downloaded and OCR-extracted (7,175 chars). Confirms the same case number, same parties, and explicit recitals: 'signatories to the 2024-2029 Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) ratified by City of Houston City Council on or about June 18, 2024, and Memorandum of Understanding dated June 11, 2024'. Section 1.3 states the parties 'agree to submit this Settlement Agreement to Arbitrator William Hartsfield to be incorporated verbatim to an order resolving the pending CBA grievances' -- and the Arbitration Award's own text (para. 9) confirms this incorporation ('Attachment 1 is the parties' settlement of those Grievances, which settlement is incorporated by this reference for all purposes as if fully set out in this Award').
caveat: Same hfdcoa.org hosting caveat as the Arbitration Award above. This document alone is a settlement of specific FY26 monetary grievances (uniform allowance, holiday buy-back, laundry funding, sick-reserve cash-out timing), not the base CBA text (recognition, full wage schedule, hours) -- it does not stand alone as 'the' Houston Fire CBA.
notes: Companion/Attachment-1 document to the Arbitration Award (same row, tx_houston_fire_2024); stored in corpus/ but the contracts.csv full_text_path points to the Arbitration Award as the primary, most complete instrument. Referenced in the row's notes/total_comp_note.

source_target: Memorandum of Understanding Regarding the Collective Bargaining Agreement 2025 through 2029, between City of Houston and HPFFA Local 341 (dated 2024-06-11)
reason: Downloaded and OCR-extracted (4,236 chars). Clarifies fiscal-impact provisions of the CBA, directly defining the escalator contingency at the heart of the Arbitration Award's Issue 1 ('the escalator percentages in Article 17, Section 2...are contingent on joint efforts by the City and the Association to seek additional new revenues'). Explicitly cited by the Arbitration Award as clarifying Article 17 Sec.2.
caveat: Same hfdcoa.org hosting caveat. This is a clarifying memorandum, not the base CBA text itself -- marked resolved_partial (found and genuine, but only a fragment/clarification of the underlying CBA, not the full agreement).
notes: Fetched as a third companion document (same tx_houston_fire_2024 row/cycle) because it is directly and repeatedly invoked by the Arbitration Award to interpret the escalator clause at issue.

source_target: Interim Amendment No. 1 to the 2025-2029 CBA (deletes Article 24 Sec.5 item d)
reason: Downloaded and OCR-extracted (759 chars); genuine 1-page procedural amendment deleting one item from Article 24 Sec.5, unrelated to the escalator/sick-reserve dispute this run is anchored on.
caveat: Narrow, single-item procedural amendment; not necessary for this run's causal row and not fetched into corpus/ as a companion (kept in scratch only, not stored).
notes: Available at the same hfdcoa.org page for a future session if a complete Article-by-Article CBA reconstruction is ever attempted.
